input:
# Microcycle — Week of 2026-02-23

**Program:** Sarah’s 3-Day Full-Body Strength + Muscle (Body Recomp) Program  
**Phase:** Phase 1: Foundation & Skill (Week 1 of 4)  
**User:** Sarah Chen  

## Schedule
- **Mon:** Full Body A (Squat emphasis) — commercial gym  
- **Tue:** Rest / optional easy walk 20–30 min  
- **Wed:** Full Body B (Hinge emphasis) — commercial gym  
- **Thu:** Rest  
- **Fri:** Full Body C (Single-leg + hypertrophy balance) — commercial gym  
- **Sat:** Rest / optional easy bike 20 min  
- **Sun:** Rest

## Week Overview
Week 1 is about establishing repeatable starting loads and crisp technique at **RPE 6–7** (you should finish most sets feeling like you could do ~3–4 more reps). Because you don’t have baseline numbers yet, the loads below are **conservative “first-week estimates”**—your job is to adjust **the same day** to stay in the RPE target. Keep rest controlled (90–150s on compounds; 45–90s on accessories) and prioritize full range of motion.

---

# Workout — Monday, February 23, 2026
**Focus:** Full Body A (Squat emphasis: squat pattern + horizontal push/pull + core)  
**Location:** Commercial gym  
**Duration:** ~65 minutes  

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. Incline treadmill walk: **5 min** (easy pace, light sweat)
2. Dynamic lower + upper prep (2 rounds):
   - Bodyweight squat to box/bench: **× 8**
   - Hip hinge drill (hands on hips, push hips back): **× 8**
   - Scapular push-ups (from knees if needed): **× 8**
   - Band row (or very light cable row): **× 12**

## Main Workout

### 1. Leg Press
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 70 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 90 lb × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 110 lb × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 4:** 110 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 5:** 110 lb × 9 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Feet mid-stance, knees track over toes, control the bottom (no bouncing). If 110 feels like RPE 8+, drop to **100** for the remaining sets.

### 2. Dumbbell Bench Press
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 15 lb DBs × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 20 lb DBs × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 20 lb DBs × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 20 lb DBs × 9 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** Set shoulder blades “down and back,” slight pause near the bottom, press to straight wrists.

### 3. Chest-Supported Row (machine or incline bench DB row)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 40 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 55 lb × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 55 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 55 lb × 9 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** Initiate by pulling elbows back; don’t shrug. Brief squeeze at the top.

### 4. Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
**Target:** 2 × 8–12 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 20 lb DBs × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 30 lb DBs × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 30 lb DBs × 9 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Soft knees, hips back, DBs close to legs, stop when hamstrings tug (neutral spine).

### 5. Cable Crunch
**Target:** 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 25 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
- **Set 2:** 25 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Exhale hard as you crunch; ribs down; don’t turn it into a hip-hinge.

### 6. Optional Finisher — Incline Treadmill Walk
**Target:** 8–10 min easy-moderate  
- **1.8–2.2 mph @ 6–10% incline**, nasal-breathing if possible  
**Notes:** This should not feel like a “workout,” just extra steps/conditioning.

## Cool Down (5 minutes)
1. Quad stretch (standing or couch stretch): **30 sec/side**
2. Chest doorway stretch: **30 sec/side**
3. Hamstring stretch: **30 sec/side**
4. Easy walk + slow breathing: **2 minutes**

## Notes
- Goal today was to find loads that land at **RPE 6–7** with clean reps—don’t chase fatigue in Week 1.
- Total time: ~65 minutes.

---

# Workout — Wednesday, February 25, 2026
**Focus:** Full Body B (Hinge emphasis: hinge + vertical pull/push + glutes)  
**Location:** Commercial gym  
**Duration:** ~70 minutes  

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. Easy bike: **5 min**
2. Prep circuit (2 rounds):
   - Glute bridge: **× 10**
   - Dead bug: **× 6/side**
   - Lat pulldown scap pulls (light): **× 8**
   - Empty trap bar holds (or light DBs): **2 × 10 sec** (practice bracing)

## Main Workout

### 1. Trap Bar Deadlift
**Target:** 3 × 5–8 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 65 lb × 5 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 85 lb × 5 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 105 lb × 6 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 4:** 105 lb × 6 (RPE 7)
- **Set 5:** 105 lb × 5 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2.5–3 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Brace first (big breath, tighten), push the floor away, stand tall at the top. If grip is limiting, use chalk if available.

### 2. Lat Pulldown (neutral or medium grip)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 40 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 55 lb × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 55 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 55 lb × 9 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** Pull elbows to pockets; pause briefly at the bottom; control the rise.

### 3. Seated DB Shoulder Press
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 10 lb DBs × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 15 lb DBs × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 15 lb DBs × 9 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 15 lb DBs × 9 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** Ribs down, don’t over-arch; press slightly back (not straight forward).

### 4. Hip Thrust (machine preferred; barbell if comfortable)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 50 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 70 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 3:** 70 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 70 lb × 9 (RPE 7.5)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Chin tucked, posterior pelvic tilt at the top, 1-second squeeze.

### 5. Leg Curl (machine)
**Target:** 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 40 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
- **Set 2:** 40 lb × 11 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 75 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Control both directions; don’t slam the stack.

### 6. Side Plank
**Target:** 2 × 20–40 sec/side (stop 1–2 reps shy of form breakdown)  
- **Round 1:** 25 sec/side  
- **Round 2:** 25 sec/side  
**Rest:** 45–60 seconds between sides/rounds  
**Notes:** Straight line head-to-heel; top hip slightly forward (avoid rotating open).

## Cool Down (5 minutes)
1. Kneeling hip flexor stretch: **30 sec/side**
2. Lat stretch (hands on rig/bench): **30 sec**
3. Figure-4 glute stretch: **30 sec/side**
4. Slow breathing (lying on bench/floor): **1 minute**

## Notes
- This is your “skill practice” deadlift day—perfect reps > heavy reps.
- Total time: ~70 minutes.

---

# Workout — Friday, February 27, 2026
**Focus:** Full Body C (Single-leg stability + extra upper volume + arms/shoulders)  
**Location:** Commercial gym  
**Duration:** ~70–75 minutes  

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. Row machine: **4 min** easy
2. Movement prep (2 rounds):
   - Goblet squat (very light): **× 8**
   - Walking lunge (bodyweight): **× 6/leg**
   - Band pull-aparts (or reverse pec deck very light): **× 12**
   - Wrist/elbow warm-up curls (very light DBs): **× 10**

## Main Workout

### 1. Goblet Squat
**Target:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 20 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 30 lb × 12 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 30 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 30 lb × 11 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Elbows inside knees, tall chest, full-foot pressure. If depth is limited, squat to a box and gradually lower over weeks.

### 2. Machine Chest Press
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 40 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 55 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 3:** 55 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 55 lb × 9 (RPE 7.5)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** Keep shoulders down; stop 1–2 cm short of locking elbows aggressively.

### 3. Cable Row (neutral grip)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 40 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 55 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 3:** 55 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 55 lb × 9 (RPE 7.5)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** Small pause with shoulder blades back; avoid leaning way back.

### 4. DB Walking Lunge
**Target:** 2 × 8–12/leg @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 15 lb DBs × 10/leg (RPE 7)
- **Set 2:** 15 lb DBs × 9/leg (RPE 7.5)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between sets  
**Notes:** Shorter stride if knees feel stressed; think “down, not forward.” Use straps-free DBs to build grip.

### 5. Lateral Raise
**Target:** 2 × 12–20 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Set 1:** 10 lb DBs × 15 (RPE 7)
- **Set 2:** 10 lb DBs × 14 (RPE 7.5)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Slight lean forward, raise to shoulder height, control down.

### 6. Triceps Pressdown (rope)
**Target:** 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Set 1:** 25 lb × 12 (RPE 7.5)
- **Set 2:** 25 lb × 11 (RPE 8)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Elbows pinned; full extension with a brief squeeze.

### 7. Dumbbell Curl
**Target:** 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Set 1:** 12.5 lb DBs × 12 (RPE 7.5)
- **Set 2:** 12.5 lb DBs × 11 (RPE 8)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Don’t swing; keep upper arm still; control the lowering.

## Cool Down (5 minutes)
1. Calf stretch (wall): **30 sec/side**
2. Pec stretch (doorway): **30 sec/side**
3. Quad stretch: **30 sec/side**
4. Child’s pose with side reach (lats): **30 sec/side**

## Notes
- Today should feel like a solid “pump + practice” day, not a max-out day.
- Total time: ~75 minutes.

---

## Weekly Summary
- **Full Body A (Squat emphasis):** Established baseline leg press and DB bench loads at controlled **RPE 6–7**; technique-first approach set you up to progress reps next week.
- **Full Body B (Hinge emphasis):** Trap bar deadlift introduced with manageable loads and full bracing practice; accessories supported back/shoulders/glutes without excessive fatigue.
- **Full Body C (Single-leg + hypertrophy):** Added single-leg work and arm/shoulder volume for balanced development and body comp support.
- **Constraint monitoring:** No issues expected; if any knee/low-back discomfort shows up, reduce ROM slightly and keep RPE closer to **6** next session.
- **Next week (Week 2):** Keep loads the same unless sets felt **≤ RPE 6**—priority is adding **+1 rep per set** on key moves (leg press, DB bench, pulldown/rows, goblet squat) while staying in the rep ranges.

## 2026-02-27 — Profile created/updated (intake)

If you share your current body weight (and optionally waist measurement) plus a few starting lift estimates (even machines are fine), I can add baseline METRICS so progress is measurable.</Profile>

<Plan>## Header
- **Program name:** Sarah’s 3-Day Full-Body Strength + Muscle (Body Recomp) Program  
- **Program Owner:** GymText  
- **User:** Sarah Chen  
- **Goal:** Build strength and muscle while improving body composition (fat loss with lean-mass retention/gain)  
- **Duration:** **Ongoing with phase cycling** (repeatable blocks)

---

## Program Philosophy
Sarah is a novice lifter training **3x/week**, so the fastest, safest progress will come from **frequent practice of the basics** (squat pattern, hinge pattern, push, pull, carry, core) with **simple progression**. This program uses a **full-body split** each day to build skill and strength efficiently while keeping weekly volume high enough for hypertrophy and body recomposition. Exercises prioritize stable, gym-friendly options (machines + dumbbells + a few barbell patterns as tolerated) to reduce technique bottlenecks early on. Progression is primarily **double progression + RPE** (adding reps first, then load), with planned deloads to keep recovery and adherence strong.

---

# Phase 1: Foundation & Skill (Weeks 1–4)

### Weekly Pattern (3 sessions/week, e.g., Mon/Wed/Fri)
> **Intensity target:** Mostly **RPE 6–7** (leave ~3–4 reps in reserve early), focus on consistent technique and repeatability.  
> **Rest:** Compounds 90–150s, accessories 45–90s.

## Day 1 — Full Body A (Squat emphasis)
- **Focus:** Squat pattern + horizontal push/pull + core
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1) **Leg Press**: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 6–7**  
   *Stable squat-pattern strength and quad stimulus without high technical demand early on.*

2) **Dumbbell Bench Press**: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 6–7**  
3) **Chest-Supported Row (machine or bench)**: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 6–7**  
   *Balances pressing volume and builds upper-back strength for shoulder health and posture.*

4) **Romanian Deadlift (DB or barbell)**: 2 × 8–12 @ **RPE 6–7**  
   *Posterior-chain strength for body comp and athletic base.*

5) **Cable Crunch**: 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**  
6) **Optional finisher:** Incline treadmill walk or bike 8–12 min @ easy-moderate (can talk in sentences)

---

## Day 2 — Full Body B (Hinge emphasis)
- **Focus:** Hinge pattern + vertical push/pull + glutes
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1) **Trap Bar Deadlift** (or **DB RDL** if preferred): 3 × 5–8 @ **RPE 6–7**  
   *Beginner-friendly hinge that builds full-body strength with manageable technique demands.*

2) **Lat Pulldown**: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 6–7**  
3) **Seated DB Shoulder Press** (or machine press): 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 6–7**  
4) **Hip Thrust (machine or barbell)**: 2–3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7**  
   *Direct glute work supports lower-body development and body composition goals.*

5) **Leg Curl (machine)**: 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7**  
6) **Side Plank**: 2 × 20–40 sec/side (stop 1–2 reps short of form breakdown)

---

## Day 3 — Full Body C (Single-leg + hypertrophy balance)
- **Focus:** Single-leg stability + extra upper volume + arms/shoulders
- **Volume:** ~15–20 working sets

1) **Goblet Squat**: 3 × 10–15 @ **RPE 6–7**  
   *Grooves squat mechanics, builds legs without needing heavy loading yet.*

2) **Machine Chest Press** (or push-ups): 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7**  
3) **Cable Row (neutral grip)**: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7**  
4) **DB Walking Lunge** (or split squat): 2 × 8–12/leg @ **RPE 7**  
   *Single-leg strength and hip stability for balanced leg development.*

5) **Lateral Raise**: 2 × 12–20 @ **RPE 7–8**  
6) **Triceps Pressdown**: 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7–8**  
7) **DB Curl**: 2 × 10–15 @ **RPE 7–8**

---

### Progression Strategy (Phase 1)
- **Main rule (double progression):** Stay within the rep range until you can hit the **top end of reps on all sets** at the target RPE, then add load next time.
  - Example: DB Bench 3×8–12  
    - Week to week aim: 8/8/8 → 9/9/9 → 10/10/10 … until 12/12/12, then add 2–5 lb per dumbbell and return to ~8 reps.
- **RPE guardrails:**  
  - If a set exceeds the target (feels like RPE 8–9), keep weight the same next week (or reduce slightly) and prioritize form.
- **Deload (Week 4):**  
  - Reduce **sets by ~40–50%** (e.g., 3 sets → 1–2 sets) and keep loads similar or slightly lighter, staying **RPE 6**.
- **Transition to Phase 2:**  
  - Move on after Week 4 if technique feels consistent and you’re progressing in reps/loads without excessive soreness or missed reps.

---

# Phase 2: Linear Build (Weeks 5–8)

### Weekly Pattern
Same **3-day full-body** structure, but slightly heavier on the first movement each day.

> **Intensity target:** Main lifts **RPE 7–8**, accessories **RPE 7–8**.  
> **Volume:** Similar weekly sets, but more “work sets” are challenging.

## Day 1 — Full Body A (Squat strength + upper)
- **Leg Press**: 4 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **DB Bench Press**: 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Chest-Supported Row**: 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **RDL (DB or barbell)**: 2–3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Cable Crunch**: 2–3 × 10–15 @ RPE 7–8

## Day 2 — Full Body B (Hinge strength + vertical)
- **Trap Bar Deadlift**: 4 × 4–6 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Lat Pulldown**: 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **DB/Machine Shoulder Press**: 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Hip Thrust**: 3 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Leg Curl**: 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 8  
- **Side Plank**: 2 × 25–45 sec/side

## Day 3 — Full Body C (Hypertrophy + single-leg)
- **Goblet Squat** (or **Hack Squat machine** if available): 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Machine Chest Press**: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Cable Row**: 3 × 8–12 @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Lunge/Split Squat**: 2–3 × 8–12/leg @ **RPE 7–8**  
- **Lateral Raise**: 2 × 12–20 @ RPE 8  
- **Triceps Pressdown**: 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 8  
- **DB Curl**: 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 8

---

### Progression Strategy (Phase 2)
- **Main lifts (first exercise each day):**  
  - Progress load **1–5%** when you complete all sets at the top of the rep range (or when RPE drops below 7 at the same reps).
- **Accessories:** keep double progression; add reps first, then small load increases.
- **Deload (Week 8):** same approach as Phase 1 deload (40–60% volume reduction, RPE ~6).
- **Transition trigger:**  
  - If progress slows (reps stall for 2 straight exposures) or fatigue is accumulating, move to Phase 3.

---

# Phase 3: Resensitization + Variety (Weeks 9–12)

### Weekly Pattern
Same 3 days/week, but slightly lighter and with small variations to keep joints fresh and re-sensitize progress.

> **Intensity target:** Mostly **RPE 6–7** with clean reps; slightly higher reps on accessories.

**Key swaps (choose based on what feels best):**
- Leg Press → **Hack Squat** or **Smith Squat to box**
- Trap Bar Deadlift → **RDL** or **Back Extension**
- DB Bench → **Incline DB Bench** or **Machine Press**
- Rows/Pulldowns stay similar, focus on quality and range

Keep overall set counts similar to Phase 1, with rep ranges biased to **8–15**.

---

### Progression Strategy (Phase 3)
- Aim to add **1–2 reps per set** week-to-week until you hit the top of the range, then add a small load increase.
- Deload is optional at the end of Week 12 if recovery is good; otherwise take a deload week before restarting Phase 1 or Phase 2.

---

## Phase Cycling
- **Default cycle:** **Foundation (4w) → Build (4w) → Resensitize (4w) → repeat**
- **What triggers transitions:**
  - **Time-based:** move phases as written every 4 weeks
  - **Performance-based:** if lifts are progressing smoothly and soreness is manageable, you can extend Phase 2 by 2–4 weeks before resensitizing
  - **Recovery-based:** if sleep/stress is high and loads feel unusually heavy for 2+ sessions, deload early and restart the next phase
- **Body composition support (minimal-addition approach):**
  - Optional: 2×/week easy cardio 10–20 minutes after lifting or on off days (kept easy so it doesn’t compromise strength progress)

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Initial program created for Sarah Chen (3x/week full-body strength + hypertrophy with phase cycling).

--- 

If you tell me (1) which 3 days you prefer to train, (2) your approximate session length (45/60/75 min), and (3) any exercises you already like/dislike, I can tighten exercise selection further and set a more precise weekly time budget.</Plan>

- Goal today was to find loads that land at **RPE 6–7** with clean reps—don’t chase fatigue in Week 1.
- Total time: ~65 minutes.

---

# Workout — Wednesday, February 25, 2026
**Focus:** Full Body B (Hinge emphasis: hinge + vertical pull/push + glutes)  
**Location:** Commercial gym  
**Duration:** ~70 minutes  

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. Easy bike: **5 min**
2. Prep circuit (2 rounds):
   - Glute bridge: **× 10**
   - Dead bug: **× 6/side**
   - Lat pulldown scap pulls (light): **× 8**
   - Empty trap bar holds (or light DBs): **2 × 10 sec** (practice bracing)

## Main Workout

### 1. Trap Bar Deadlift
**Target:** 3 × 5–8 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 65 lb × 5 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 85 lb × 5 (warm-up)
- **Set 3:** 105 lb × 6 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 4:** 105 lb × 6 (RPE 7)
- **Set 5:** 105 lb × 5 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2.5–3 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Brace first (big breath, tighten), push the floor away, stand tall at the top. If grip is limiting, use chalk if available.

### 2. Lat Pulldown (neutral or medium grip)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 40 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 55 lb × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 55 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 55 lb × 9 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** Pull elbows to pockets; pause briefly at the bottom; control the rise.

### 3. Seated DB Shoulder Press
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 10 lb DBs × 10 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 15 lb DBs × 10 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 15 lb DBs × 9 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 15 lb DBs × 9 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** Ribs down, don’t over-arch; press slightly back (not straight forward).

### 4. Hip Thrust (machine preferred; barbell if comfortable)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 50 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 70 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 3:** 70 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 70 lb × 9 (RPE 7.5)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Chin tucked, posterior pelvic tilt at the top, 1-second squeeze.

### 5. Leg Curl (machine)
**Target:** 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 40 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
- **Set 2:** 40 lb × 11 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 75 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Control both directions; don’t slam the stack.

### 6. Side Plank
**Target:** 2 × 20–40 sec/side (stop 1–2 reps shy of form breakdown)  
- **Round 1:** 25 sec/side  
- **Round 2:** 25 sec/side  
**Rest:** 45–60 seconds between sides/rounds  
**Notes:** Straight line head-to-heel; top hip slightly forward (avoid rotating open).

## Cool Down (5 minutes)
1. Kneeling hip flexor stretch: **30 sec/side**
2. Lat stretch (hands on rig/bench): **30 sec**
3. Figure-4 glute stretch: **30 sec/side**
4. Slow breathing (lying on bench/floor): **1 minute**

## Notes
- This is your “skill practice” deadlift day—perfect reps > heavy reps.
- Total time: ~70 minutes.

---

# Workout — Friday, February 27, 2026
**Focus:** Full Body C (Single-leg stability + extra upper volume + arms/shoulders)  
**Location:** Commercial gym  
**Duration:** ~70–75 minutes  

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. Row machine: **4 min** easy
2. Movement prep (2 rounds):
   - Goblet squat (very light): **× 8**
   - Walking lunge (bodyweight): **× 6/leg**
   - Band pull-aparts (or reverse pec deck very light): **× 12**
   - Wrist/elbow warm-up curls (very light DBs): **× 10**

## Main Workout

### 1. Goblet Squat
**Target:** 3 × 10–15 @ RPE 6–7  
- **Set 1:** 20 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 30 lb × 12 (RPE 6.5)
- **Set 3:** 30 lb × 12 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 30 lb × 11 (RPE 7)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Elbows inside knees, tall chest, full-foot pressure. If depth is limited, squat to a box and gradually lower over weeks.

### 2. Machine Chest Press
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 40 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 55 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 3:** 55 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 55 lb × 9 (RPE 7.5)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** Keep shoulders down; stop 1–2 cm short of locking elbows aggressively.

### 3. Cable Row (neutral grip)
**Target:** 3 × 8–12 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 40 lb × 12 (warm-up)
- **Set 2:** 55 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 3:** 55 lb × 10 (RPE 7)
- **Set 4:** 55 lb × 9 (RPE 7.5)
**Rest:** 90 seconds between working sets  
**Notes:** Small pause with shoulder blades back; avoid leaning way back.

### 4. DB Walking Lunge
**Target:** 2 × 8–12/leg @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 15 lb DBs × 10/leg (RPE 7)
- **Set 2:** 15 lb DBs × 9/leg (RPE 7.5)
**Rest:** 2 minutes between sets  
**Notes:** Shorter stride if knees feel stressed; think “down, not forward.” Use straps-free DBs to build grip.

### 5. Lateral Raise
**Target:** 2 × 12–20 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Set 1:** 10 lb DBs × 15 (RPE 7)
- **Set 2:** 10 lb DBs × 14 (RPE 7.5)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Slight lean forward, raise to shoulder height, control down.

### 6. Triceps Pressdown (rope)
**Target:** 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Set 1:** 25 lb × 12 (RPE 7.5)
- **Set 2:** 25 lb × 11 (RPE 8)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Elbows pinned; full extension with a brief squeeze.

### 7. Dumbbell Curl
**Target:** 2 × 10–15 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Set 1:** 12.5 lb DBs × 12 (RPE 7.5)
- **Set 2:** 12.5 lb DBs × 11 (RPE 8)
**Rest:** 60 seconds between sets  
**Notes:** Don’t swing; keep upper arm still; control the lowering.

## Cool Down (5 minutes)
1. Calf stretch (wall): **30 sec/side**
2. Pec stretch (doorway): **30 sec/side**
3. Quad stretch: **30 sec/side**
4. Child’s pose with side reach (lats): **30 sec/side**

## Notes
- Today should feel like a solid “pump + practice” day, not a max-out day.
- Total time: ~75 minutes.

---

## Weekly Summary
- **Full Body A (Squat emphasis):** Established baseline leg press and DB bench loads at controlled **RPE 6–7**; technique-first approach set you up to progress reps next week.
- **Full Body B (Hinge emphasis):** Trap bar deadlift introduced with manageable loads and full bracing practice; accessories supported back/shoulders/glutes without excessive fatigue.
- **Full Body C (Single-leg + hypertrophy):** Added single-leg work and arm/shoulder volume for balanced development and body comp support.
- **Constraint monitoring:** No issues expected; if any knee/low-back discomfort shows up, reduce ROM slightly and keep RPE closer to **6** next session.
- **Next week (Week 2):** Keep loads the same unless sets felt **≤ RPE 6**—priority is adding **+1 rep per set** on key moves (leg press, DB bench, pulldown/rows, goblet squat) while staying in the rep ranges.</Week>

# Week 1 Microcycle Dossier (2026-02-23 → 2026-03-01)

**Athlete:** Sarah Chen (Novice)  
**Program:** Sarah’s 3-Day Full-Body Strength + Muscle (Body Recomp) Program  
**Phase:** **Phase 1 — Foundation & Skill (Week 1 of 4)**  
**Theme / Focus:** Establish conservative starting loads + repeatable technique at **RPE 6–7** (leave ~3–4 reps in reserve).  
**Rest Targets:** Compounds **90–150s** (deadlift up to **2.5–3 min**), accessories **45–90s**  
**Conditioning (optional):** Easy incline walk / easy bike as noted (keep conversational)

---

## Schedule (Planned vs. Completed)
- **Mon:** Full Body A (Squat emphasis) — **Completed**
- **Tue:** Rest / optional easy walk 20–30 min
- **Wed:** Full Body B (Hinge emphasis) — **Completed**
- **Thu:** Rest
- **Fri:** Full Body C (Single-leg + hypertrophy balance) — **Completed**
- **Sat:** Rest / optional easy bike 20 min
- **Sun:** Rest

---

## Week-at-a-Glance (Key Metrics)
**Session durations:** ~65 min (Mon) / ~70 min (Wed) / ~75 min (Fri)

### Working Set Volume (counting “working” sets only; warm-ups excluded)
- **Mon:** ~**12** working sets  
- **Wed:** ~**13** working sets *(includes side plank rounds)*  
- **Fri:** ~**16** working sets  
- **Estimated total:** ~**41** working sets across the week

### Intensity (RPE)
- Most work landed **RPE 6.5–7.5**  
- Highest reported: **RPE ~8** on isolation (pressdowns/curls) and some late sets

### Baseline Loads Established (top working sets)
- **Leg Press:** **110 × 10/10/9** @ RPE 6.5–7  
- **DB Bench Press:** **20s × 10/10/9** @ RPE 6.5–7  
- **Chest-Supported Row:** **55 × 10/10/9** @ RPE 6.5–7  
- **DB RDL:** **30s × 10/9** @ RPE 6.5–7  
- **Trap Bar Deadlift:** **105 × 6/6/5** @ RPE 6.5–7  
- **Lat Pulldown:** **55 × 10/10/9** @ RPE 6.5–7  
- **Seated DB Shoulder Press:** **15s × 10/9/9** @ RPE 6.5–7  
- **Hip Thrust:** **70 × 10/10/9** @ RPE 7–7.5  
- **Goblet Squat:** **30 × 12/12/11** @ RPE 6.5–7  
- **DB Walking Lunge:** **15s × 10/leg, 9/leg** @ RPE 7–7.5  
- **Cable Row:** **55 × 10/10/9** @ RPE 7–7.5  

---

## Monday (Feb 23) — Full Body A (Squat Emphasis)
**Focus:** Squat pattern + horizontal push/pull + core  
**Duration:** ~65 min  
**Warm-up:** 5 min incline walk + 2 rounds dynamic prep

### Main Lifts & Accessories (Working Sets)
1) **Leg Press** — **110 lb: 3×(10,10,9)** @ RPE 6.5–7  
2) **DB Bench Press** — **20s: 3×(10,10,9)** @ RPE 6.5–7  
3) **Chest-Supported Row** — **55 lb: 3×(10,10,9)** @ RPE 6.5–7  
4) **DB Romanian Deadlift** — **30s: 2×(10,9)** @ RPE 6.5–7  
5) **Cable Crunch** — **25 lb: 2×12** @ RPE 7  
6) **Optional Finisher:** Incline treadmill **8–10 min** @ easy-moderate

**Key notes:** Technique-first, controlled depth on leg press; keep RPE capped (drop to ~100 if 110 trends toward RPE 8+).

---

## Wednesday (Feb 25) — Full Body B (Hinge Emphasis)
**Focus:** Hinge pattern + vertical pull/push + glutes  
**Duration:** ~70 min  
**Warm-up:** 5 min bike + 2 rounds prep (bridges/dead bug/scap pulls/bracing holds)

### Main Lifts & Accessories (Working Sets)
1) **Trap Bar Deadlift** — **105 lb: 3×(6,6,5)** @ RPE 6.5–7  
2) **Lat Pulldown** — **55 lb: 3×(10,10,9)** @ RPE 6.5–7  
3) **Seated DB Shoulder Press** — **15s: 3×(10,9,9)** @ RPE 6.5–7  
4) **Hip Thrust (machine)** — **70 lb: 3×(10,10,9)** @ RPE 7–7.5  
5) **Leg Curl** — **40 lb: 2×(12,11)** @ RPE 7  
6) **Side Plank** — **2 rounds × 25 sec/side**

**Key notes:** “Skill practice” deadlift day—bracing and consistent reps prioritized over load; grip support allowed (chalk).

---

## Friday (Feb 27) — Full Body C (Single-Leg + Hypertrophy Balance)
**Focus:** Single-leg stability + additional upper volume + arms/shoulders  
**Duration:** ~70–75 min  
**Warm-up:** 4 min row + 2 rounds movement prep (light goblet/lunges/pull-aparts)

### Main Lifts & Accessories (Working Sets)
1) **Goblet Squat** — **30 lb: 3×(12,12,11)** @ RPE 6.5–7  
2) **Machine Chest Press** — **55 lb: 3×(10,10,9)** @ RPE 7–7.5  
3) **Cable Row (neutral)** — **55 lb: 3×(10,10,9)** @ RPE 7–7.5  
4) **DB Walking Lunge** — **15s: 2×(10/leg, 9/leg)** @ RPE 7–7.5  
5) **Lateral Raise** — **10s: 2×(15,14)** @ RPE 7–7.5  
6) **Triceps Pressdown (rope)** — **25 lb: 2×(12,11)** @ RPE 7.5–8  
7) **DB Curl** — **12.5s: 2×(12,11)** @ RPE 7.5–8  

**Key notes:** “Pump + practice” day; maintain clean reps and control on isolation work as RPE edges up.

---

## Progression & Takeaways (Week 1 → Week 2)
### What progressed this week
- Established **repeatable baseline loads** for all main movement patterns (squat, hinge, push, pull, single-leg, core).
- RPE largely stayed in the correct training zone for Phase 1.

### Week 2 Progression Targets
- **Keep loads the same** unless a movement felt **≤ RPE 6** across all working sets.  
- Primary goal: add **~+1 rep per set** on key lifts while staying within rep ranges:
  - **Leg Press, DB Bench, Rows/Pulldown, Goblet Squat**
- If any set drifts to **RPE 8–9**, hold load steady (or slightly reduce) and prioritize form/ROM.

---

## Notes & Considerations
- **Constraint monitoring:** If **knees or low back** feel irritated, reduce ROM slightly, tighten bracing, and keep sessions closer to **RPE 6** next exposure.
- **Recovery emphasis:** This is Week 1—avoid chasing fatigue; consistency and crisp reps are the priority.
